questions.q: What was Dushasana's place among Dhritarashtra's hundred sons?
questions.explanation: Dushasana was the second of the hundred Kauravas, right after Duryodhana.

questions.q: What infamous act did Dushasana commit in the dice hall?
options: He stole the royal dice mid-game
He dragged Draupadi in by her hair
He struck Yudhishthira across the face
He set fire to the assembly doors
questions.explanation: On Duryodhana's orders, Dushasana hauled Draupadi into the assembly by her hair.

questions.q: What did Dushasana attempt after hauling Draupadi before the court?
options: To auction her before the kings
To brand her as a slave
questions.explanation: Dushasana laid hands on Draupadi's sari and tried to disrobe her before the assembled kings.

questions.q: By whose grace did Draupadi's sari grow endless as Dushasana pulled at it?
questions.explanation: Tradition credits Krishna with the miracle, though some recensions attribute it to dharma itself.

questions.q: Who vowed in the dice hall to drink Dushasana's blood?
questions.explanation: Bhima swore before the whole court that he would tear open Dushasana's chest and drink his blood.

questions.q: On which day of the Kurukshetra war did Dushasana die?
questions.explanation: Dushasana fell on the seventeenth day, the same day Karna later died.

questions.q: Who killed Dushasana on the battlefield?
questions.explanation: Bhima struck him down and fulfilled the vow sworn in the dice hall years before.

questions.q: What did Bhima do after tearing open Dushasana's chest?
options: He flung the body before Karna
He carried the corpse to Draupadi
He scattered the remains to crows
He drank the blood as he had vowed
questions.explanation: Bhima drank Dushasana's blood on the field, exactly as he had sworn in the hall of dice.

questions.q: Whose most eager instrument was Dushasana among the Kauravas?
questions.explanation: Dushasana carried out his elder brother Duryodhana's cruelest commands without hesitation.

questions.q: At which event did Dushasana drag Draupadi before the assembled kings?
questions.explanation: The outrage took place in the assembly hall of Hastinapura after Yudhishthira lost the dice game.

questions.q: Who ordered that Draupadi and the Pandavas be stripped of their garments?
questions.explanation: It was Karna who commanded the clothes be taken, and Dushasana who seized Draupadi's sari.

questions.q: In what state was Draupadi when Dushasana dragged her to court?
options: Dressed in royal finery for a feast
Asleep in the inner garden pavilion
Clad in a single cloth during her courses
Performing worship at the palace shrine
questions.explanation: She was in seclusion, wearing a single garment during her monthly courses, which made the outrage greater.

questions.q: Bhima's vow against Dushasana was paired with which vow against Duryodhana?
options: To behead him before his father
To drown him in the Ganga
To shatter his thighs with the mace
To burn his palace to ash
questions.explanation: In the same hall Bhima swore to drink Dushasana's blood and to smash Duryodhana's thighs.

questions.q: Inside the Chakravyuha, Dushasana was battered unconscious in a duel with which young hero?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu knocked Dushasana senseless inside the wheel formation; later Dushasana's son struck the boy's death-blow.

questions.q: In popular retellings, what vow did Draupadi keep until Dushasana's death?
options: To leave her hair unbound until it was washed in his blood
To eat only once a day while he lived
To speak no word inside any royal court
To wear only white until vengeance came
questions.explanation: The hair vow is famous from later tradition such as the play Venisamhara; Vyasa's text stresses Bhima's vow.

questions.q: Which Kaurava brother protested Dushasana's treatment of Draupadi in the hall?
questions.explanation: Vikarna alone argued that Draupadi had not been lawfully staked, but Karna shouted him down.

questions.q: What question did Draupadi put to the hall as Dushasana held her?
options: Whether Yudhishthira had lost himself before staking her
Whether Shakuni's dice had been fairly thrown
Whether a king could wager his brothers at all
Whether Bhishma would grant her safe passage home
questions.explanation: She asked whether a man who had already lost himself could stake his wife, and the elders sat silent.

questions.q: How did Dushasana mock the Pandavas as they left for exile?
options: He rattled Shakuni's dice in their faces
He danced around them bellowing cow, cow
He paraded their crowns through the streets
He read out the terms of exile aloud
questions.explanation: Dushasana capered around Bhima shouting cow, cow, a taunt Bhima never forgot.

questions.q: What moved Dhritarashtra to grant Draupadi boons after her humiliation by Dushasana?
options: Bhishma's threat to leave the court
Gandhari's vow to fast in protest
Krishna's sudden arrival in the hall
Fearful omens like jackal cries filled the city
questions.explanation: Jackals howled and omens multiplied until the frightened king freed the Pandavas with boons.

questions.q: Per widely told accounts, what did Bhima tear from Dushasana's body besides opening his chest?
options: The arm that had dragged Draupadi
The royal sash of the Kauravas
questions.explanation: Later retellings and stage versions show Bhima ripping off the arm that had seized Draupadi's hair; the epic itself stresses the blood-drinking.

questions.q: Who was first sent to summon Draupadi before Dushasana went himself?
questions.explanation: Draupadi sent the usher Pratikamin back with her question, so the enraged Duryodhana dispatched Dushasana.

questions.q: From where did Dushasana drag Draupadi to the assembly?
options: From the courtyard of the guest house
From the women's inner quarters of the palace
From her chariot at the palace gates
questions.explanation: He stormed into the women's quarters where she was secluded and hauled her out by the hair.

questions.q: Who delivered the final mace blow that killed Abhimanyu inside the Chakravyuha?
questions.explanation: Dushasana's son, called Durmasena in some texts, clubbed the exhausted Abhimanyu down in the mace fight.

questions.q: Who commanded the Kaurava army on the day Dushasana fell to Bhima?
questions.explanation: Dushasana died on the seventeenth day, when Karna led the Kaurava host.

questions.q: To what did Bhima compare the taste of Dushasana's blood?
options: Ganga water after a desert march
The soma of the sacrificial altars
A draught sweeter than mother's milk
questions.explanation: Bhima declared the blood sweeter than mother's milk or honey, terrifying all who watched.

questions.q: How did Bhishma answer Draupadi's question as Dushasana held her captive?
options: He said the course of dharma was too subtle to judge
He declared the stake void and freed her
He commanded Dushasana to release her at once
He asked Drona to pronounce the verdict
questions.explanation: Bhishma admitted that dharma was too subtle for him to decide, and the outrage went unchecked.

questions.q: At the Virata cattle raid, which disguised warrior routed Dushasana and the Kuru host?
options: Bhima disguised as the cook Vallabha
questions.explanation: Arjuna, still in his guise as Brihannala, single-handedly scattered the Kuru army including Dushasana.

questions.q: How did Dushasana answer Draupadi's plea that she was unfit to appear in court?
options: He blamed Yudhishthira for staking her
He jeered that a slave woman had no such privileges
He swore he was only following royal orders
He promised she would be freed after the game
questions.explanation: Calling her a slave won at dice, Dushasana laughed that a dasi could claim no such modesty.

questions.q: How did Karna justify the disrobing that Dushasana carried out?
options: A defeated king's goods belonged to the victor
The dice had made the Pandavas slaves beyond appeal
He declared a woman with five husbands no chaste wife
He claimed the gods had sanctioned the game's result
questions.explanation: Karna sneered that a woman with five husbands was no chaste wife and ordered the garments taken.

questions.q: What does the name Dushasana roughly mean?
questions.explanation: From duh and shasana, the name suggests one who is ill-ruled or difficult to govern.
